name: mathjax-full
when_to_use: For rendering LaTeX mathematical equations as SVG. Use the pre-built MathEquation component instead of importing mathjax-full directly
api_reference:
MATHJAX — LaTeX EQUATION RENDERING

Do NOT import mathjax-full directly. Use the pre-built <MathEquation> component which wraps mathjax-full for synchronous SVG rendering.

```tsx
<MathEquation
  latex="E = mc^2"
  theme={theme}
  fontSize={60}
  color="#58a6ff"
  label="Einstein's equation"
  revealMode="fade"  // 'fade' | 'clip' | 'scale'
  startFrame={15}
/>
```

The latex prop takes raw LaTeX (no \[ \] delimiters needed).

For inline math within text, wrap the equation in $...$ and handle rendering via the MathEquation component.

PREFER using <LaTeX> from mafs when equations are inside a coordinate system or math visualization context.
forbidden_patterns: Do NOT import from 'mathjax-full' directly — use the MathEquation component
Do NOT use better-react-mathjax — it renders asynchronously which breaks screenshot capture
Do NOT add \[ \] or $ delimiters to the latex prop — pass raw LaTeX only
remotion_integration_notes: MathEquation renders LaTeX synchronously to SVG using mathjax-full. This ensures frame-perfect rendering for screenshots and visual review.
